  "chain": [

    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go", "symbol": "GetMigrations"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go", "symbol": "Migrate"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go", "symbol": "V2"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v36.go", "symbol": "V36"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "public/app/features/dashboard/state/DashboardMigrator.ts", "symbol": "DashboardMigrator"}
  ],
  "text": "The dashboard JSON schema version migration pipeline in grafana/grafana is implemented across two layers.\n\n**Backend (Go) — apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/**\n\nThe migration runner is `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`. It exposes a singleton `migrator` struct with `Initialize(dsInfoProvider)` (which calls `GetMigrations` to build the migration map) and `Migrate(ctx, dash, targetVersion)` (which sequentially applies each versioned migration function from the current schema version up to the target). Before running migrations it applies frontend defaults via `applyFrontendDefaults` (implemented in `frontend_defaults.go`) and after running them it adds a built-in annotation query and cleans up the save model.\n\nThe versioned migration function registry is in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go`. The function `GetMigrations(dsInfoProvider DataSourceInfoProvider) map[int]SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` returns a map from integer schema version to migration function, covering versions 2 through 42 (LATEST_VERSION). Each map entry references a function named `V{N}` defined in a separate file.\n\nIndividual migration steps live in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go` through `v42.go`. Notable examples:\n- `V2` (v2.go): migrates legacy `services.filter` to top-level `time` and `templating.list`.\n- `V36` (v36.go): migrates datasource references from legacy name strings to structured UID-based objects.\n\nSupporting files include `datasource_utils.go`, `migration_utils.go`, and `errors.go` in the schemaversion package, plus `metrics.go` at the migration package level.\n\nThe `conversion/` subdirectory (`conversion.go`, `v0.go`, `v1.go`, `v2.go`, `v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`, `errors.go`, `metrics.go`) handles API-version-level conversion between the versioned Kubernetes-style dashboard API objects (v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1).\n\n**Frontend (TypeScript)**\n\nThe frontend mirror of the backend migration logic is `public/app/features/dashboard/state/DashboardMigrator.ts`, which exports the `DashboardMigrator` class. This class implements the same schema-version-by-version migration logic in TypeScript, used when dashboards are loaded directly by the Grafana frontend without going through the backend migration pipeline."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Grafana Dashboard Schema Version Migration Pipeline

## Your Task

Find all Go and TypeScript source files in grafana/grafana that implement the dashboard JSON schema version migration pipeline. Identify: the migration runner (apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/), the versioned migration function registry (GetMigrations returning version-to-function map), individual migration steps (e.g. V2, V36), and the frontend DashboardMigrator in TypeScript that mirrors the backend logic.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.


## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
Now let me check the TypeScript DashboardMigrator and a few key migration files:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the complete list of files:
Now I have everything needed to create the answer:

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of what was found:

## Dashboard Schema Version Migration Pipeline in grafana/grafana

### Backend Migration Runner
**`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`** — The singleton `migrator` struct. `Initialize()` builds the migration map; `Migrate(ctx, dash, targetVersion)` applies frontend defaults then runs each versioned migration function from the current version up to the target.

### Versioned Migration Registry
**`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go`** — Contains `GetMigrations(dsInfoProvider) map[int]SchemaVersionMigrationFunc`, returning a map of version integers (2–42) to their migration functions `V2`…`V42`.

### Individual Migration Steps
**`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go` through `v42.go`** — Each file implements one `V{N}` function. Examples:
- `V2`: migrates legacy `services.filter` structure
- `V36`: migrates datasource references from name strings to UID-based structured objects

### Supporting Infrastructure
- `schemaversion/datasource_utils.go`, `migration_utils.go`, `errors.go` — shared utilities
- `frontend_defaults.go` — applies frontend defaults before migrations run
- `conversion/` — handles API-version-level conversion between Kubernetes-style versioned dashboard objects (v0alpha1 → v1beta1 → v2alpha1 → v2beta1)

### Frontend TypeScript Mirror
**`public/app/features/dashboard/state/DashboardMigrator.ts`** — The `DashboardMigrator` class mirrors the backend migration logic in TypeScript, used when dashboards are loaded directly by the frontend.

[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/workspace/grafana--26d36ec/apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go", "content": "package schemaversion\n\nimport (\n\t\"strconv\"\n\n\t\"golang.org/x/net/context\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\tMIN_VERSION    = 0\n\tLATEST_VERSION = 42\n)\n\ntype SchemaVersionMigrationFunc func(context.Context, map[string]interface{}) error\n\ntype DataSourceInfo struct {\n\tDefault    bool\n\tUID        string\n\tName       string\n\tType       string\n\tID         int64\n\tAPIVersion string\n}\n\ntype DataSourceInfoProvider interface {\n\t// GetDataSourceInfo returns a list of all data sources with their info\n\t// The context must have the namespace in it\n\tGetDataSourceInfo(ctx context.Context) []DataSourceInfo\n}\n\ntype PanelPluginInfo struct {\n\tID      string\n\tVersion string\n}\n\nfunc GetMigrations(dsInfoProvider DataSourceInfoProvider) map[int]SchemaVersionMigrationFunc {\n\treturn map[int]SchemaVersionMigrationFunc{\n\t\t2:  V2,\n\t\t3:  V3,\n\t\t4:  V4,\n\t\t5:  V5,\n\t\t6:  V6,\n\t\t7:  V7,\n\t\t8:  V8,\n\t\t9:  V9,\n\t\t10: V10,\n\t\t11: V11,\n\t\t12: V12,\n\t\t13: V13,\n\t\t14: V14,\n\t\t15: V15,\n\t\t16: V16,\n\t\t17: V17,\n\t\t18: V18,\n\t\t19: V19,\n\t\t20: V20,\n\t\t21: V21,\n\t\t22: V22,\n\t\t23: V23,\n\t\t24: V24,\n\t\t25: V25,\n\t\t26: V26,\n\t\t27: V27,\n\t\t28: V28,\n\t\t29: V29,\n\t\t30: V30,\n\t\t31: V31,\n\t\t32: V32,\n\t\t33: V33(dsInfoProvider),\n\t\t34: V34,\n\t\t35: V35,\n\t\t36: V36(dsInfoProvider),\n\t\t37: V37,\n\t\t38: V38,\n\t\t39: V39,\n\t\t40: V40,\n\t\t41: V41,\n\t\t42: V42,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc GetSchemaVersion(dash map[string]interface{}) int {\n\tif v, ok := dash[\"schemaVersion\"]; ok {\n\t\tswitch v := v.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\treturn v\n\t\tcase float64:\n\t\t\treturn int(v)\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\tif version, err := strconv.Atoi(v); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn version\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn 0\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn 0\n}\n", "numLines": 98, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 98}}

  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go", "symbol": "GetMigrations"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go", "symbol": "GetSchemaVersion"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go", "symbol": "SchemaVersionMigrationFunc"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go", "symbol": "DataSourceInfoProvider"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go", "symbol": "Migrate"},

    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go", "symbol": "V2"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v36.go", "symbol": "V36"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "public/app/features/dashboard/state/DashboardMigrator.ts", "symbol": "DashboardMigrator"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go", "symbol": "Initialize"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go", "symbol": "GetMigrations"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go", "symbol": "Migrate"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go", "symbol": "V2"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v36.go", "symbol": "V36"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "public/app/features/dashboard/state/DashboardMigrator.ts", "symbol": "DashboardMigrator"}
  ],
  "text": "The dashboard JSON schema version migration pipeline in grafana/grafana is implemented across two layers.\n\n**Backend (Go) — apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/**\n\nThe migration runner is `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`. It exposes a singleton `migrator` struct with `Initialize(dsInfoProvider)` (which calls `GetMigrations` to build the migration map) and `Migrate(ctx, dash, targetVersion)` (which sequentially applies each versioned migration function from the current schema version up to the target). Before running migrations it applies frontend defaults via `applyFrontendDefaults` (implemented in `frontend_defaults.go`) and after running them it adds a built-in annotation query and cleans up the save model.\n\nThe versioned migration function registry is in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go`. The function `GetMigrations(dsInfoProvider DataSourceInfoProvider) map[int]SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` returns a map from integer schema version to migration function, covering versions 2 through 42 (LATEST_VERSION). Each map entry references a function named `V{N}` defined in a separate file.\n\nIndividual migration steps live in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go` through `v42.go`. Notable examples:\n- `V2` (v2.go): migrates legacy `services.filter` to top-level `time` and `templating.list`.\n- `V36` (v36.go): migrates datasource references from legacy name strings to structured UID-based objects.\n\nSupporting files include `datasource_utils.go`, `migration_utils.go`, and `errors.go` in the schemaversion package, plus `metrics.go` at the migration package level.\n\nThe `conversion/` subdirectory (`conversion.go`, `v0.go`, `v1.go`, `v2.go`, `v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`, `errors.go`, `metrics.go`) handles API-version-level conversion between the versioned Kubernetes-style dashboard API objects (v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1).\n\n**Frontend (TypeScript)**\n\nThe frontend mirror of the backend migration logic is `public/app/features/dashboard/state/DashboardMigrator.ts`, which exports the `DashboardMigrator` class. This class implements the same schema-version-by-version migration logic in TypeScript, used when dashboards are loaded directly by the Grafana frontend without going through the backend migration pipeline."
}

  "text": "The dashboard JSON schema version migration pipeline in grafana/grafana is implemented across two layers.\n\n**Backend (Go) — apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/**\n\nThe migration runner is `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`. It exposes a singleton `migrator` struct with `Initialize(dsInfoProvider)` (which calls `GetMigrations` to build the migration map) and `Migrate(ctx, dash, targetVersion)` (which sequentially applies each versioned migration function from the current schema version up to the target). Before running migrations it applies frontend defaults via `applyFrontendDefaults` (implemented in `frontend_defaults.go`) and after running them it adds a built-in annotation query and cleans up the save model.\n\nThe versioned migration function registry is in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go`. The function `GetMigrations(dsInfoProvider DataSourceInfoProvider) map[int]SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` returns a map from integer schema version to migration function, covering versions 2 through 42 (LATEST_VERSION). Each map entry references a function named `V{N}` defined in a separate file.\n\nIndividual migration steps live in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go` through `v42.go`. Notable examples:\n- `V2` (v2.go): migrates legacy `services.filter` to top-level `time` and `templating.list`.\n- `V36` (v36.go): migrates datasource references from legacy name strings to structured UID-based objects.\n\nSupporting files include `datasource_utils.go`, `migration_utils.go`, and `errors.go` in the schemaversion package, plus `metrics.go` at the migration package level.\n\nThe `conversion/` subdirectory (`conversion.go`, `v0.go`, `v1.go`, `v2.go`, `v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`, `errors.go`, `metrics.go`) handles API-version-level conversion between the versioned Kubernetes-style dashboard API objects (v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1).\n\n**Frontend (TypeScript)**\n\nThe frontend mirror of the backend migration logic is `public/app/features/dashboard/state/DashboardMigrator.ts`, which exports the `DashboardMigrator` class. This class implements the same schema-version-by-version migration logic in TypeScript, used when dashboards are loaded directly by the Grafana frontend without going through the backend migration pipeline."
}
